zh.lifeSummary: 當Roger Bacon III於1310年出生在英國諾福爾可巴根斯索普時，他的父親Henry Bacon25歲，母親Margaret Ludham22歲。他於1339年與Felicia Kirton在英國英格蘭諾福爾可巴根斯索普結婚。他們至少是5個女兒的父母。他去世於1394年在英國英格蘭奧可福爾享年84歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Roger Bacon III于1310年出生在英国諾福爾可巴根斯索普时，他的父亲Henry Bacon25岁，母亲Margaret Ludham22岁。他于1339年与Felicia Kirton在英国英格兰諾福爾可巴根斯索普结婚。他们至少是5个女儿的父母。他去世于1394年在英国英格兰奧可福爾享年84岁。

nameMeanings.name: Bacon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin) and French: from the Norman French personal name Bacun, derived from the ancient Germanic name Bac(c)o, Bahho, based on the element bag ‘(to) fight, (to) dispute’. The name was relatively common among the Normans in the form Bacus, of which the oblique case was Bacon.

English and French: from Middle English, Old French bacun, bacon ‘bacon’ (a word of ancient Germanic origin, akin to Back 3), probably a metonymic occupational name for a preparer and seller of cured pork.

History: Gilles Bacon from Normandy, France, is documented in Quebec City, QC, in 1647. — Michael Bacon from England arrived in Dedham, MA, in 1640. Nathanial Bacon, from Stratton, Cornwall, arrived in Barnstaple, MA, in 1639. Another Nathaniel Bacon (1647–76), from Friston Hall, Suffolk, emigrated to VA and settled at Curl's Neck on the James river.

variants.name: Bagg
meanings: from Middle English bagge ‘bag, pack, bundle’, hence a metonymic occupational name for a maker of bags and sacks of various kinds, including wallets and purses. It might also have been used for a beggar, or for one who carried their belongings in a bag.
from the ancient Germanic personal name Bago (Bac(c)o, Bahho), from the root bag- ‘to fight’ (see Bacon 2).

variants.name: Back
meanings: English (Devon, Kent, Sussex, and Norfolk): from the Middle English personal name Bakke (Old English Bacca). It is of uncertain origin, but may have been a byname in the same sense as 3.
English: nickname from Middle English bakke ‘bat’, of uncertain application, perhaps a nickname for a person with poor eyesight, from the expression ‘blind as a bat’.

English: from Middle English bakke ‘back’ (Old English bæc), hence a nickname for someone with a hunched back or some other noticeable peculiarity of the back or spine, or a topographic name for someone who lived on a hill or ridge, or at the rear of a settlement.

nameMeanings.name: Roger
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French personal name, Rog(i)er, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, from hrōd ‘fame’ + gār, gēr ‘spear’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain, replacing the native Old English form Hrōðgār. Roger was one of the most popular boys' names throughout the medieval period, but less so after the Reformation, though it has continued in regular use to the present day. Roger, Count of Sicily ( c.1031–1101 ), son of Tancred, recovered Sicily from the Arabs. His son, also called Roger, ruled Sicily as king, presiding over a court noted for its splendour and patronage of the arts.

variants.name: Rodger
meanings: Variant spelling of   Roger  , in part from the surname derived from the given name in the Middle Ages.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Roger Bacon III was born in 1310, in Baconsthorpe, Norfolk, England, his father, Henry Bacon, was 25 and his mother, Margaret Ludham, was 22. He married Felicia Kirton in 1339, in Baconsthorpe, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 5 daughters. He died in 1394, in Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 84.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Roger Bacon III est né en 1310, à Baconsthorpe, Norfolk, Angleterre, son père, Henry Bacon, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Margaret Ludham, avait 22 ans. Il a épousé Felicia Kirton en 1339, à Baconsthorpe, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 5 filles. Il est décédé en 1394, à Oxfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 84 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Roger Bacon III 1310 in Baconsthorpe, Norfolk, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Henry Bacon 25 und seine Mutter Margaret Ludham 22. Er hat 1339 in Baconsthorpe, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom Felicia Kirton geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Töchter. Er starb 1394 im Alter von 84 Jahren in Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Roger Bacon III è nato il nel 1310 nel Baconsthorpe, Norfolk, England, il suo padre, Henry Bacon, aveva 25 anni e il suo madre, Margaret Ludham, aveva 22 anni. Lui sposato Felicia Kirton nel 1339, nel Baconsthorpe, Norfolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figlie. Morì nel 1394, nel Oxfordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 84 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Roger Bacon IIIが1310年にイギリスノーフォルクバコンスソルペで生まれた時，父Henry Baconは25歳，母Margaret Ludhamは22歳であった。1339年にイギリスイギリスノーフォルクバコンスソルペでFelicia Kirtonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の娘の親であった。彼は1394年にイギリスイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアで84歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Roger Bacon III(이)가 1310년 영국 노르폴크 바콘스소에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Henry Bacon(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret Ludham(은)는 22세였다. 그는 1339년 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 바콘스소에서 Felicia Kirton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1394년 영국 잉글랜드 스퍼드실르에서 84세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Roger Bacon III nasceu em 1310, em Baconsthorpe, Norfolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, Henry Bacon, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Margaret Ludham, tinha 22 anos. Ele casou-se com Felicia Kirton em 1339, em Baconsthorpe, Norfolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhas. Ele faleceu em 1394, em Oxfordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 84 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Roger Bacon III родился в 1310 г. в местности бакунстаурп, норфолк, Англия, его отцу по имени Henry Bacon было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret Ludham, 22. Он женился на женщине по имени Felicia Kirton в 1339 г. в местности бакунстаурп, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 5 дочерей. Он умер в 1394 г. в местности оксфордшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 84 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Roger Bacon III nació en 1310, en Baconsthorpe, Norfolk, Inglaterra, su papá, Henry Bacon, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Margaret Ludham, tenía 22 años. Se casó con Felicia Kirton en 1339, en Baconsthorpe, Norfolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijas. Murió en 1394, en Oxfordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 84 años.
